prompt: /perspective state management — the app uses a mix of Redux Toolkit for global state and React Context for theme/auth. Some components also use local useState for form data. I want to know if this pattern is coherent or if we've got overlapping concerns

assertions.text: A perspective report markdown file exists in the outputs directory
Report is narrowed to state management — does not broadly audit unrelated areas like deployment, testing, or styling
Report evaluates the coherency of using Redux Toolkit + React Context + useState together — identifies whether responsibilities overlap or are well-separated
Report references current React or Redux Toolkit documentation (via Context7 or web search) for recommended state management patterns
Report contains a clear recommendation on whether to consolidate, keep the current split, or restructure — using one of: Stay the course, Adjust, Pivot, or Adopt
Report contains checkbox action items specifically about state management improvements (not generic advice)
